config.linter.run: When to run the xbsllint linter for .xbsl.
config.linter.run.enum.0: Lint as you type (debounced) and on save
config.linter.run.enum.1: Lint only on save and open
config.linter.run.enum.2: No automatic linting (only the "XBSL: check the whole project" command)
config.linter.command: Linter executable (on PATH or an absolute path). Ignored when xbsl.linter.pythonPath is set.
config.linter.pythonPath: Path to a Python interpreter. When set, the linter is invoked as "<python> -m xbsllint" (the xbsllint package must be installed into that interpreter).
config.linter.dataDir: The Element data root (a directory with index.json). Empty – the linter resolves it itself (the XBSLLINT_DATA_DIR env, an external data package, the bundle).
config.linter.lang.enum.0: As the linter resolves it (env / system locale / ru)
config.linter.select: Check only these rules (comma-separated: id, group or tier letter A/B/C/D). Empty – the default set.
config.linter.ignore: Exclude these rules (comma-separated: id, group or tier letter).
config.linter.debounce: Delay in milliseconds before linting as you type (the onType mode).
config.workspaceLint: On every save of a .xbsl/.yaml file run the full linter pass over the workspace folder and lay the findings over all files. Brings the cross-file rules (code/unknown-type, yaml/unknown-type and others) that a single-buffer check cannot see.
config.workspaceLintTimeout: Timeout of the full workspace run in milliseconds (0 – no limit). On expiry the linter process is stopped and a message goes to the XBSL log.
config.navigation.enabled: Go to definition and completion over the project index (the "xbsllint index" command). If the installed linter does not support the index, navigation just stays silent.
config.projectRoot: Sources root for project-wide runs and the navigation index: a path relative to the workspace folder (or absolute). Empty – the whole workspace folder. Set it when the repository holds examples or copies next to the project: otherwise project-scope rules (Ид uniqueness and others) cross-fire between directories.
config.lsp.enabled: Experimental: work through the long-living xbsllint-lsp server (needs the linter with the [lsp] extra: pip install "xbsllint[lsp]"). Brings hover and instant diagnostics; when the server is unavailable the extension falls back to the regular mode by itself. Toggling requires a window reload.
config.lsp.command: The LSP server launch command. Empty – xbsllint-lsp from PATH, or `<xbsl.linter.pythonPath> -m xbsllint.lsp` when the interpreter is set. Example: C:/Python/Scripts/xbsllint-lsp.exe.
